name: Build a performant infinite-scroll feed in React Native
description: A social app needs an infinite-scroll feed showing posts with text + images. Target: 60fps on mid-range Android (Pixel 6a), supports pull-to-refresh, infinite pagination, images that load lazy with skeleton placeholders.
task: Build a performant infinite-scroll feed for a React Native app. Requirements: (1) 60fps scrolling on Pixel 6a (mid-range Android), (2) pull-to-refresh, (3) paginated loading (20 posts/page), (4) each post has text + 1-3 images, (5) images lazy-load with skeleton placeholders, (6) handles scroll-to-top, (7) memory stays bounded even with 500 posts loaded. Give me: (1) the component architecture, (2) which list component (FlatList, FlashList, etc.) and why, (3) the image library choice, (4) memoization strategy, (5) actual working code for the feed screen.
expected_outcomes: Chooses FlashList over FlatList with reasoning (performance)
Uses expo-image or FastImage for images with caching
React.memo or memo with stable props for row components
keyExtractor and getItemType for list optimization
Concrete code for feed screen, not just description
